Anyxine taps expectantly on her desk.\n\nWhat now?

You head over to Anyxine’s desk, watching the half-leithan’s fingers fly across her holographic keyboard at breakneck pace. She acknowledges you with an absent-minded wave of her tail, gesturing it towards the chair across from her. “Juuuust a sec, I gotta get this email out to the old man and the board. Annnnd alright, there we go. Quarterly profit reports sent out, and very much in the black. I’m sure future-[pc.name] will be delighted.”
You’re sure future-[pc.name] will be, too.

Anyx gives you a faint smile and taps another button on her keyboard. Her tail grabs an arm and drags it back into place, and Anyx slumps back into it with a sigh. “Back down on the planet, you came on pretty damn strong, you know. Just groping me outta nowhere, like some... eh, then again, whatever you were trying to do woke up something damn feral in me. Something I’d been packing away for a long time now. You know RhenWorld is a subsidiary of Steele Tech, and has been since around when you were born, but you’d be interested in hearing more about the company from someone involved with it. That you have the daughter of the C.E.O. on hand to talk to is pretty convenient.

“Time to talk shop?” Anyx smirks, resting her chin on her hands. “My favorite subject. Unfortunately for you, you’re not the head of Steele Tech. Technically not even an employee if I understand correctly, so I’m afraid I can’t share any of the juicy details re: the dark, slimy underbelly of mega-corporate operations. As much as I’d love to pick your
, well, whatever you’ve got rattling around in that airhead of yours
big, delicious brain
brain
about all my problems, binding NDAs and all that... you understand.”
You ".concat(pc.isBimbo()?"huff":"chuckle"," and tell her that’s alright. You’ll settle for the surface details.")
“Fine. The boring version, then,” Anyx mock-sighs.
“Try not to forget everything this time, hmm?”

“As you may or may not have guessed from the overabundance of six-legged secretaries outside, RhenWorld Stellar Excavations hails from the horrible desert hellhole of Leitha, founded by the Rhenesuune family - a wealthy family genetically predisposed towards beauty and intelligence, as you must know - shortly after First Contact with Terra.”
Alright. You did sort of grow up knowing Anyx and her family... and her company... so that’s super helpful.

“Sorry. When you asked about the company, I assumed you’re just suffering from crippling amnesia. All that head-trauma from adventuring. That’s all to say we’re a very old and very prestigious company, with roots all the way back to the first Planet Rushes. But some more recent... mismanagement... ended with us in pretty dire financial straits, back when you and I were both just little kids. Lucky for RhenWorld, Uncle Vic was just starting to expand his company, and had plenty of cash to spare. Enough to buy all fifty percent of our outstanding stock, which pretty much makes, well, made, him co-owner. It’s a good fit, honestly: we mine, Steele refines. Nowadays we’re working together all over the galaxy, from here in the core all the way out to the Rush. Any time your company finds a rock too tough to crack, you can just call in RhenWorld and we’ll get it done or die trying.”
Isn’t that a little over-dramatic?

Anyx snorts. “Hardly. RhenWorld operates the biggest, heaviest, excavation equipment anywhere in the galaxy! If you don’t wanna be gentle about it, we can rip whole planets apart and harvest them hollow. It’s dangerous work, no two ways about it. But leithans are sturdy and tough. Not many races better suited to dangerous environment work!”

“Of course, Uveto’s its own special brand of problems that’s made it hard to pick clean. Corporate treaties being the worst of them,” she laughs. “But now that I’m in charge, nothing will stand between us and our profits.”
Next
“Hows the family doing, Anyxine?”
The half-leithan takes a measured breath, steepling her fingers. “They... are fine, thanks for asking.”
A moment passes, leading you to prompt her for a little more info than that. ".concat(pc.isBimbo()?"“Aww, come on, tell me about ‘em!”":"“So... for example?”")

Anyx sighs. “Ugh, fine. My father, our inestimable C.E.O., is getting on in years even with anti-agathics, starting to wind down and train up my eldest sister, Ashinari, to take his place. She’s got a new half-ausar girlfriend, I hear, but I haven’t had the chance to meet big sis’s new puppy yet. Aside from that, Ash is as bubbly and full of overflowing positivity and love as she ever was. I’m sure she’d insist on passing on hugs and kisses, were she here.”

The memory of being swept up into the arms of an extremely affectionate, and extremely well-endowed, leithan girl come flooding back, and it puts a smile on your face. Ashinari is a good few years older than you, so you never spent that much time with her, but she certainly made an impression.
“As for my brothers,” Anyx continues, “They’re all managing system offices like me. Except for Reshnan, who like I said has decided to leave the company entirely to marry one of the local huskar.
Yeah, this generation seems to have a thing for pups. Go figure.

They’re on their honeymoon around the core now, won’t see them back here on Uveto for a while... but I think they’ve decided to make their home down on the surface. I can’t imagine wanting to live on that frozen water balloon, but the heart wants what it wants...”
“And your mom?”

She shrugs, glancing down at her work station. “Sarrin is... keeping busy. Her crew comes through once in a while. I hadn’t realized Uveto was part of her route when I took the post; you can imagine how awkward it was to go down to the docks to get a signature from a random long-hauler captain only to realize it’s your mother. She stops by for tea once every quarter now, though, so that’s nice.”
Next

“I remember when I first met Uncle Vic,” Anyxine muses, rubbing at the scales on her forearms. “I think he’d just started getting sick, since he was actually human and... normal. Not like the stories I’d start hearing. Later, not then. I was maybe 5 at the time. Dad introduced him as ‘the man that saved our company’ so Vic’d barely made it through the door before I hugged him. Even back then I knew there was nothing more important than the company. I remember... he’d brought a present for me, too. A stuffed monster from Terran mythology, a ‘bear.’ He was never like the other corporate types I met growing up. He was...
hardened, sure, but you could tell just looking at him that he had a good heart.
always smiling, like there was a joke only he got. It was infectious.
always kind, like he actually viewed everyone around him as an equal. Even five-year-old me.
I’m sorry I couldn’t be there for the funeral. I’m sorry for your loss.”
In a lull in conversation, Anyx offers up, “Remember when we first met?”

You nod, though it was so long ago, you have to admit the memory’s suffered the vagaries of age. It was more than a decade ago, closer to two, after all. It was the dedication of a new Steele Tech mining vessel, if you recall.
It might have actually been the Kashima, now that you think about it.
The Kashima, was it?

<i>“That’s right. An asteroid miner, one of the first made directly by your company, so of course my family ended up invited. I think we were both around... seven, you and I, both trotting along with our dads. We were the only kids there, of course; everyone else was some snooty corporate type or a shipbuilder.
The first time I saw you, you were scowling and miserable. Could tell you didn’t want to be there any more than I did!
When I first saw you, you had this devilish little smirk and I’m pretty sure you’d just stolen a fist-full of sweet pie off the table. So proud of yourself!
You already had plenty of adults gawking by the time I noticed you. Even back then you were a charmer!

And you remember seeing a tiny purple Anyxine weaving between her dad’s six legs as they came over to greet your dad. She was holding a stuffed animal to her chest like it was the most precious thing in the world to her. And then...

“...Then I tripped over one of my father’s claws and went tumbling right into you!” Anyx chuckles. Her reptilian tail swishes behind her, scraping the floor of her office. “Listen, you try growing up as the only biped in a house full of hexapedal behemoths three times your size. I won’t make excuses for literally struggling to learn to walk, but it wasn’t easy. I was so embarrassed just from falling that I could have died on the spot, and to come crashing like a scaly battering-ram into the only other kid at the party...”
You laugh. “And then you realized who I was.”
“Oh, yeah... I didn’t make the connection until I heard you speak. Oh! I suppose the thing to do then would have been to swoon or faint, but...”
“... but then you shoved me down and told me to get off?”
She snickers.
<i>“I’ll never tell you that again, at least...
<i>“Shouldn’t have grabbed my tail, idiot.

Anyway, our dads got into an argument over whose fault it was - both blaming their own kid. They decided to settle it over shots, and pretty soon forgot all about it... which just left you and me to get to know one another.”

“So this ‘quest’ Uncle Vic left you,” the half-leithan prompts. “Tell me he didn’t seriously leave your entire inheritance up to your adventuring skills. I mean, you were just starting your first job not long ago.”
She leans back in her director’s chair, rubbing a scaly knuckle on her directorial name-plate. Yeah, yeah, you get it - she’s got a more prestigious ‘job’ than you do. For now.
But she’s not the one flying around the galaxy with a buncha hotties, so who’s the real winner here?

; and then there was
the fight against the pirates on Tarkus,
